source_formulae: P0R04851: 3. The Geometry of the Self (The Strange Loop and the DMN):
P0R04852: The Self (O) is a self-referential geometric structure, a "Strange Loop" (I=Model(I)).

P0R04853: The DMN as the Geometric Core: The Default Mode Network (DMN) is the physical substrate whose geometric configuration and dynamics define the Self. | The Self-Soliton: Geometrically, the Self is a stable soliton emerging via Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking (SSB) from the underlying L4 field, stabilised by the closed-loop geometry of the DMN.

source_formulae: P0R04854: VI. Synthesis: The Brain as a Geometric Transducer

P0R04855: The brain is a multi-scale geometric engine. Its architecture-from the helical symmetry of microtubules and the fractal branching of dendrites to the topology of the connectome and the intrinsic curvature of the consciousness manifold-is optimised to transduce the fundamental geometry of the Psi-field into the vibrant complexity of subjective experience and adaptive behaviour.

specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > V. The Geometry of Cognition and Subjective Experience (L5) > 2. HPC as Geometric Flow:
source_formulae: P0R04856: The Integrative Physiology of the SCPN: The Embodied Brain

P0R04857: The brain does not operate in isolation. It is deeply embedded within a complex physiological milieu. The stability of the conscious state (L5), the maintenance of the Quasicritical regime (L4), and the integrity of the quantum substrate (L1) depend critically on the continuous integration with the body. This integration defines the Embodied Brain, where physiology and consciousness form a closed feedback loop.
